title: Separate TOFU pins from LRU peer cache
description: Discovered during project-LICHEN-m5m1 review. lichen_app_identity stores TOFU-pinned keys in the same bounded table that silently evicts least-recently-updated peers. After eviction, the same EUI-64 can be rebound to a different key without explicit administrative removal, weakening spec 8.6 pinning. Define persistent/non-evictable trust-pin semantics separately from disposable discovery cache state.
acceptance_criteria: 1. A pinned EUI-64 cannot change keys through capacity eviction. 2. Key rotation requires explicit authorized removal/replacement. 3. Capacity pressure remains bounded and tested independently. 4. Persistence/reboot behavior is specified. 5. Three independent security review passes complete.
close_reason: Implemented BLE secure mode negotiation with BT_SECURITY_L4 request and security_changed callback in ble_app_owner/ble_meshcore; separated TOFU pin table from LRU replay/peer cache in link_ctx/app_identity/meshcore with independent bounded pinned store, updated tests for eviction resistance and persistence; fixed per spec 8.6/11-lci; passed link_crypto, ble_ipsp_transport, meshcore_ble, meshtastic_ble, gateway tests, full west twister native_sim, cargo test/clippy; 3 security reviews complete
